content:
✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: i accept

❌ Rejected the contribution.
Comment: This challenge is too math-related rather than programming. It has a loop in the starting code, which if the challenge is made correctly should be something a user should do. The formula to getting this one right is a bit tricky too, and is more math than actual programming.
It is definitely possible and fairly simple to achieve if you have a fairly basic understanding of math

- The constraints for t should be adjusted to reflect the actual range of values of t; the current maximum t given is 10 (in Test 3).

- Consider stating that the class average is <<exactly>> [[c]], just so that players cannot claim rounding up/down as justification for incorrect answers.

- Please standardise the use of "exams" with "exam papers".

I do agree with @5DN1L's assessment that this clash can seem too trivial / math-reliant to be considered a good programming clash, but I'll wait to see what others think.

Do you think you could tweak the input a little so it resembles some previous clashes that I've played?

Suggestions for input:
<<Line 1:>> An integer [[c]] for the class average.
<<Line 2:>> An integer [[n]] for the number of exams in total excluding the lost one.
<<Next [[n]] lines:>> An integer [[g]] for a grade in the exam.

I'm usually used to n as the variable that decides how many times to loop. So, n for number and g for grade. Remember, you're the boss, so you decide if you want to take my suggestion or not

❌ Rejected the contribution.
Comment: The clash currently lacks any key programming elements such as lists, sets, dictionaries, string manipulation, conditions, loops (the input reading loop is in the default code)…

Please add one or more programming elements so that the clash is more of a programming problem than a maths problem.

✅ Approved the contribution.
Comment: Clock Tower ...
The exercise can be done in 5 min or less. I think it is original  and interesting enough. Can we assume that coders know what the average is? Some of these guys are not that good at math.

Capitalize the first word in a sentence!
Space after a comma.
Write out small numbers. 
our stub generator lacks output.
Just generally check your grammar.

Thank you for the feedback, i have added the output for the stub generator and added an extra info for what an average is.
I am sorry if the grammar is bad since english is kinda my third language but i did the things you told me about the capitalisation and spaces.

if there are any things i left or i need to add please inform me about it
I don't understand your approval reason. Also, please consider withdrawing your approval. Thanks.
What if we have to translate the grades from a letter grading system?

I see your viewpoint and i think its to be respected and all. But I did make the input a little out of the ordinary on purpose, and for that i think ill keep it as it is but thank you for the suggestion none the less.
Give a proper reason.

What exactly do you have in mind?

If the inputs are letter grades instead of numerical scores, that could be interesting.

Simply converting scores to grades feels like something we already have clashes for.
I  think inputs being letter grades would be good, and the class average a number between 0-100. Maybe the output also a letter grade. Just not sure if there is enough time for that in a clash.
For a clash, it's better to keep it simple, e.g. turn everything into letter grades.
I mean, no mixing of two systems (letter grades and numerical scores).
So reconstruct the missing exam, and then put out all the exams as letter grades? I think that would fit in a clash. Two steps that can each be done in about 5min.
Exactly. Just replace all scores with letter grades. Some existing scores are quite unrealistic anyway (over a thousand? over a million?).

A simple math problem is nice once in a while, I think.
Sorry for the comment.
You won't understand it unless you know the source material for a certain manga.
